summary: Captured 5 App Store screenshots for the 6.3" device class (iPhone 17 native, 1206x2622) into automation/proofs/app-store-screenshots-6.3/. Parameterized the existing capture_app_store_screenshots.py with --device-name and --output-subdir args; the default invocation still produces the 6.9" set under the original output path. Added DEVICE_LOGICAL_SIZE table + tab_coords_for(width, height) helper so any iPhone simulator with known logical size can drive the same 5-surface capture flow.
validations_passed: file automation/proofs/app-store-screenshots-6.3/*.png -> PNG image data, 1206 x 2622, 8-bit/color RGBA (all 5)
design_decisions: Parameterized rather than forked. One script with --device-name + --output-subdir args; the defaults preserve the original 6.9" flow so existing callers (manual or future Makefile target) keep working without changes.

Computed tab coordinates from device logical size via tab_coords_for(width, height) instead of hard-coding per device. Five evenly-spaced tab centers with y just above the home indicator works on any iPhone with a 5-tab tab bar.

Did not move the existing 6.9" output to automation/proofs/app-store-screenshots-6.9/. The original directory is the 'canonical' bucket Apple expects to populate the 6.9" carousel; existing references stay intact.
behavior_proven: All 5 PNGs are 1206x2622 8-bit RGBA — exact App Store 6.3" target.
Tab navigation works on iPhone 17 with the computed coords (Write/Train/Home/Today tap targets resolved correctly per the script run output).
make architecture + make automation-check + make pyright + git diff --check all green.
gaps_named_for_follow_up: App Store Connect also lists 6.5" (1284x2778) and 6.1" (1170x2532) buckets. Apple auto-downscales 6.9" -> 6.7"/6.5" and 6.3" -> 6.1" for newer apps, so the 2 captured sets cover the modern iPhone roster. If a future Apple change requires explicit 6.5" / 6.1" uploads, the same parameterized script handles it by passing a different --device-name.
iPad bucket (12.9" / 11") not captured. Owlory presumably ships iPhone-only; if iPad shipping is added, the script needs a width/height entry for iPad logical sizes (e.g., 1024x1366 for 12.9" iPad).
not_claimed: These PNGs are the screenshots that will ship. The user uploads to App Store Connect and may re-roll any.

Tab coordinates work on every iPhone simulator. The computed center+bottom-y is reliable for current iPhone shapes; an iPhone SE-style design with a different tab bar position would need explicit overrides.
On-device rendering matches simulator byte-for-byte. Real-device output may have subtle font/spacing differences.
residual_risks: If iPhone 17's logical size changes in a future iOS update (Apple has done this for some devices), DEVICE_LOGICAL_SIZE needs updating. The script will fail with a clear 'Unknown device' message rather than producing wrong coordinates.
The tab coordinate calc assumes a 5-tab tab bar with home-indicator clearance ~36pt. A future Owlory redesign that adds/removes a tab or moves the bar would invalidate the coords.
recommended_next_reason: Queue empty. Named follow-ups remain: on-device VoiceOver+Switch Control+Voice Control verification, localization translation-quality reclassification for the 95 voicecontrol entries, extend input labels to lower-frequency commands, workflow templates in manifest, real third-party repo migration.
